evals.prompt: When the user taps a highlight in my MatrixScan AR app, print the barcode data. File: IntegratedBarcodeArWithProviderViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill adds BarcodeArViewUIDelegate conformance, implements the tap callback to read barcode.data and print it, and assigns barcodeArView.uiDelegate. Existing highlight provider and integration code are preserved.

assertions.text: BarcodeArViewUIDelegate conformance is present
barcodeArView.uiDelegate = self is assigned
A delegate method with didTapHighlightFor is implemented
Inside the tap callback, barcode.data is read (directly or via optional binding)
print( is used to output the barcode data in the tap callback
DispatchQueue.main.async is NOT used inside the tap callback (the UI delegate already runs on the main queue)
Existing BarcodeArHighlightProvider conformance is preserved
Existing highlight(for:completionHandler:) method is preserved
Existing BarcodeArRectangleHighlight construction is preserved
Existing integration code (context, BarcodeAr, barcodeArView, lifecycle, highlightProvider assignment) is preserved

evals.prompt: When a highlight is tapped in my MatrixScan AR app, if the barcode data is '123456789' show a UIAlertController saying 'Rejected'. Otherwise navigate by calling showDetails(for: barcode.data ?? ""). File: IntegratedBarcodeArWithProviderViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill adds BarcodeArViewUIDelegate conformance with branching logic on barcode.data inside the tap callback, assigns uiDelegate, and preserves the existing highlight provider.

assertions.text: BarcodeArViewUIDelegate conformance is present
barcodeArView.uiDelegate = self is assigned
A didTapHighlightFor callback is implemented
The tap callback branches on barcode.data == "123456789" or equivalent
UIAlertController is constructed in the rejection branch
'Rejected' appears as the alert text
self.present( is called to show the alert
showDetails(for:) is called in the non-rejection branch
Existing BarcodeArHighlightProvider conformance is preserved
Existing integration code (context, BarcodeAr, barcodeArView, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: My MatrixScan AR view has no highlights yet. I want circular highlights that are tappable — when a user taps one, log the barcode data. File: IntegratedBarcodeArViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill adds BOTH a BarcodeArHighlightProvider (returning BarcodeArCircleHighlight) AND a BarcodeArViewUIDelegate, assigning both highlightProvider and uiDelegate on the existing barcodeArView.

assertions.text: BarcodeArHighlightProvider conformance is present
BarcodeArCircleHighlight( is used to construct the highlight
preset: is passed to BarcodeArCircleHighlight (a documented preset value)
barcodeArView.highlightProvider = self is assigned
BarcodeArViewUIDelegate conformance is present
barcodeArView.uiDelegate = self is assigned
A didTapHighlightFor callback is implemented
The tap callback logs barcode.data (via print or NSLog)
Existing integration code (context, BarcodeAr, barcodeArView, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: How do I handle taps on MatrixScan AR highlights in iOS?
evals.expected_output: The skill answers by naming BarcodeArViewUIDelegate as the interaction hook, points out that it is assigned to BarcodeArView.uiDelegate, mentions the didTapHighlightFor callback, and includes a link to the official Scandit iOS docs. No code is written to any file.

assertions.text: The response names BarcodeArViewUIDelegate
The response mentions BarcodeArView.uiDelegate (or uiDelegate as the property to assign)
The response references the tap callback (didTapHighlightFor or similar, as documented on the BarcodeArView API page)
The response does NOT invent a non-existent listener or delegate type (e.g. BarcodeArHighlightListener, BarcodeArTapDelegate, BarcodeArInteractionDelegate, onHighlightTap)
The response does NOT instruct the user to add a UITapGestureRecognizer as the primary approach (the uiDelegate route is the documented one)
The response includes a link to the Scandit iOS documentation (scandit.com/sdks/ios or scandit.com/data-capture-sdk/ios)
No files are written or edited

evals.prompt: In my SwiftUI MatrixScan AR app, log the barcode data when a highlight is tapped. File: IntegratedBarcodeArWithProviderSwiftUIView.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill adds BarcodeArViewUIDelegate conformance on the wrapped UIKit ScanViewController (not on the SwiftUI View), assigns barcodeArView.uiDelegate = self inside ScanViewController, and implements the tap callback to log barcode.data. The SwiftUI wrapper types are left intact.

assertions.text: BarcodeArViewUIDelegate conformance is added on ScanViewController (not on the SwiftUI View or the UIViewControllerRepresentable)
barcodeArView.uiDelegate = self is assigned inside the ScanViewController (e.g. in setupRecognition)
A didTapHighlightFor callback is implemented on ScanViewController
The tap callback logs barcode.data (via print or NSLog)
The SwiftUI ScanView struct is preserved (no Scandit API calls added to it)
The ScanViewControllerRepresentable struct is preserved (makeUIViewController/updateUIViewController unchanged structurally)
Existing BarcodeArHighlightProvider conformance is preserved
Existing highlight(for:completionHandler:) method is preserved
Existing integration code (context, BarcodeAr, barcodeArView, lifecycle, highlightProvider assignment) is preserved
